name: Sumatran Rhino
desc: Critically endangered due to habitat loss and fragmentation.
threats: Poaching and habitat loss threaten the Sumatran rhinoceros. Many are killed for their horns, while forest destruction isolates small groups, making breeding difficult and reducing their population.

name: Mountain Gorilla
desc: Found in high-altitude forests, facing threats from human conflict.
habitat: Cloud forests of the Virunga Mountains
threats: Mountain gorillas face threats from illegal hunting, human diseases, and conflicts near their habitats, which damage forests and reduce their population.

name: Blue Whale
desc: The largest animal ever known to have lived on Earth, migrating across global oceans.
habitat: Antarctic Ocean, Atlantic Ocean, and Pacific Ocean basins
threats: Commercial whaling historically devastated their numbers. Today, they face severe threats from ship strikes, ocean noise pollution, and toxic chemical runoffs.

name: Javan Rhino
desc: The most threatened of the five rhino species, with only one wild population remaining.
habitat: Ujung Kulon National Park in Java, Indonesia
threats: The Javan rhino has an extremely small population, making it vulnerable to disease outbreaks and natural disasters that could quickly threaten its survival.

name: Amur Leopard
desc: An incredibly rare solitary leopard adapted to living in harsh, snowy climates.
habitat: Temperate forests of the Russian Far East and Northeast China
threats: Amur leopards are threatened by illegal hunting and forest loss, while their small population increases inbreeding and reduces genetic diversity.

name: Hawksbill Turtle
desc: Highly valued for their beautiful shells, these marine turtles are vital for coral reefs.
habitat: Tropical oceans, predominantly around coral reefs
threats: Hawksbill turtles are hunted for their shells, while beach development and egg collection threaten their nesting and survival.
